body_txt: The protagonist has been happily living with their family until one day, out of the blue, they learn that the people who raised them are not in fact their biological parents, either by adoption or by infidelity. There begins the gene hunt, to find their genetic parents. This may result in lots of Freudian Excuses , You're Not My Father and "are my adoptive parents my real parents" Wangst ! or they may still be Happily Adopted and simply curious about their genetic parents. Often the result of Parental Abandonment or Raised by Wolves . There are a few different ways this can play out: Can lead to Luke, I Am Your Father , Luke, You Are My Father , Family Relationship Switcheroo , Tell Me About My Father , or Mysterious Parent situations. It could be that they don't find their parent(s), but their best friend, resulting in Missing Parents' Best Friend . If this happens in a Sitcom , be wary when Status Quo Is God . If the seeker is horrified or disappointed by their discovered ancestors, see also Disappointing Heritage Reveal . If they discover their birth parents (who want them back regardless of the adoptive parents' wishes), this may lead to an Adoption Conflict . Sub-Trope of Searching for the Lost Relative . Named for, but unrelated to, the Life on Mars (2006) character Gene Hunt. Compare Quest for the Rest . Should not be confused with Stalker with a Test Tube , an entirely different form of gene hunting. Or with using someone's DNA to find them, as is done in the CSI franchise. Has nothing to do with the Gene Hunt Interrogation Technique (or so we hope), nor hunting guys named Gene (which is generally frowned upon).
